code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a formatting lambda (subject, object) -> phrase
    predicate_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {o}th pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {o}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{o} owns {s}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the chairman of {s}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the general manager of {s}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an ethnic group of {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {o} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {o}",
    }

    # Build phrases, grouping repeated subjects for certain predicates (e.g., club)
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.predicate)
        grouped.setdefault(key, []).append(t.object)

    sentences = []
    for (subj, pred), objects in grouped.items():
        if pred in {"club", "currentclub"} and len(objects) > 1:
            # List multiple clubs
            objs = ", ".join(objects[:-1]) + f", and {objects[-1]}" if len(objects) > 2 else " and ".join(objects)
            phrase = f"{subj} plays for {objs}"
        else:
            # Use the first object for simple predicates
            obj = objects[0]
            phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)
        sentences.append(phrase)

    # Combine sentences into a single fluent paragraph
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0] + "."
    # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last clause
    *head, tail = sentences
    combined = ", ".join(head) + ", and " + tail
    return combined + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Athlete. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. (Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    sentences = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object

        # Simple natural‑language mappings for common predicates
        if p == "birthDate":
            phrase = f"{s} was born on {o}"
        elif p == "birthYear":
            phrase = f"{s} was born in {o}"
        elif p == "birthPlace":
            phrase = f"{s} was born in {o}"
        elif p == "height":
            phrase = f"{s} is {o} meters tall"
        elif p == "weight":
            phrase = f"{s} weighs {o}"
        elif p == "position":
            phrase = f"{s} plays as a {o}"
        elif p == "club" or p == "currentclub":
            phrase = f"{s} plays for {o}"
        elif p == "formerTeam":
            phrase = f"{s} formerly played for {o}"
        elif p == "league":
            phrase = f"{s} competes in the {o}"
        elif p == "manager":
            phrase = f"{s} is managed by {o}"
        elif p == "coach":
            phrase = f"{s} is coached by {o}"
        elif p == "draftYear":
            phrase = f"{s} was drafted in {o}"
        elif p == "draftRound":
            phrase = f"{s} was selected in round {o}"
        elif p == "draftPick":
            phrase = f"{s} was the {o}th pick"
        elif p == "playerNumber":
            phrase = f"{s} wears number {o}"
        elif p == "currentteam":
            phrase = f"{s} currently plays for {o}"
        elif p == "debutTeam":
            phrase = f"{s} debuted with {o}"
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            phrase = f"{s} {p} {o}"

        sentences.append(phrase)

    # Join the individual clauses into one coherent sentence
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
